_note: Keep in sync with decky-proton-pulse scoring.ts WEIGHTS and algorithm
_deprecated_note_scoreTiers: scoreTiers (was a score-to-tier mapping) is removed. Rating is never derived from a numeric score; it always comes from deriveRating(yes/no answers). See model.rating + perGameAggregation.rating for the current behavior.
formula: (rating + recency + customProton + protonMatch + playtimeConfidence) * gpuMult * osMult * kernelMult + notesSentiment + sourcePenalty
sourcePenaltyNote: Native Pulse reports (source='user') receive SOURCE_PULSE_PENALTY=-5 until the Pulse report base grows enough to match ProtonDB's confidence. ProtonDB CDN and live reports receive no penalty. See Architecture.md for details.
playtimeConfidenceNote: Reports that declare actual playtime earn a confidence bonus applied inside the GPU/OS/kernel multiplier bracket. Meaningful confidence starts at 2h (oneToFourHours=+6). Buckets: underOneHour=+3, oneToFourHours=+6, fourToTenHours=+9, overTenHours=+12. Reports with no playtime (unreported/empty) receive no bonus.

tiers.name: platinum
tiers.rule: Can install, start, and play. No faults. Works out of the box without any tinkering.

tiers.name: gold
tiers.rule: Can install, start, and play. No faults. Works but required tinkering (launch options, config, etc.).

tiers.name: silver
tiers.rule: Can install, start, and play. Exactly 2 faults reported (e.g. graphical + performance).

tiers.name: bronze
tiers.rule: Can install, start, and play. 3 or more faults. Playable but with significant issues.

tiers.name: borked
tiers.rule: Cannot install, cannot start, cannot begin playing, or overall verdict is No.

outOfBoxNote: Only shown when verdict is Yes and no faults are reported. Answering Yes elevates Gold to Platinum.
quickReference: Can't play or verdict No  = Borked
durationAutoFillNote: When submitting from the plugin, duration is pre-filled from your actual playtime. Time logged with the active config is weighted 1.25x over total Steam playtime -- hours with that exact setup matter more than lifetime hours across all configs.

ratingDerivation._note: Canonical algorithm in src/lib/scoring.ts:deriveRating(). Mirrors ProtonDB's inferLiveRating exactly. Rating is never chosen manually -- always derived from structured form answers.
ratingDerivation._source: src/lib/scoring.ts deriveRating (canonical) -- imported by NativePulseReportModal and protondb.ts inferLiveRating

installAndStartup._note: If any of these are 'no', the report is immediately borked regardless of other answers.

ratingDerivation.tinkeringNote: Tinkering (GE proton, winetricks, etc.) does not cap the rating by itself. A tinker report can still reach platinum if verdictOob='yes' (the user confirmed it also works without tinkering).

model._note: Proton Pulse tracks two distinct numbers per report and per game: a RATING (the badge) and a CONFIDENCE (how reliable that rating is).

rating.definition: Derived purely from the report's yes/no answers. Nothing else changes it - not your hardware, not how old the report is. Same report yields the same rating for everyone.
rating.perGame: Per-game rating is the recency-weighted mean of per-report ratings. Active games where recent reports trend platinum will read platinum, even if old borked reports exist.

confidence.definition: A 0-100% qualifier shown next to the badge. Confidence answers: 'how much should I trust this rating for my situation?' Lower confidence does not change the badge - it just adds a caveat next to it.
confidence.perGame: Per-game confidence is a function of sample size, freshness of the newest report, and consensus among recent reports. The plugin and signed-in webui add hardware-match factors on top.

exampleLines: GOLD - 78% confidence - based on 12 recent reports, hardware closely matches yours
PLATINUM - 92% confidence - 30+ reports in the last year, broad consensus
BORKED - 14% confidence - based on a 4-year-old report, the game has been updated since

confidenceFactors._note: Each group below maps onto entries in WEIGHTS. Together they form the per-report confidence. Anonymous web visitors drop the hardware-match groups; signed-in plugin/webui sessions use everything.

groups.label: Recency
groups.description: Newer reports are more reliable. Old reports lose ground; very old ones become weakly informative.

groups.label: Hardware match
groups.description: When you're signed in or running the plugin, reports from hardware close to yours count more. GPU vendor, driver, kernel, and OS all contribute.

groups.label: Proton version match
groups.description: Reports from the same Proton version (or close) carry more weight. Custom Proton builds (GE, CachyOS, TKG) get a small additional credit for going off the official path.

groups.label: Playtime depth
groups.description: Reports declaring real playtime earn confidence. The longer the session, the bigger the boost. Reports with no playtime stay neutral.

groups.label: Notes quality
groups.description: Free-form notes swing confidence within +/- NOTES_MAX based on positive/negative keywords. Short or vague notes have little effect.

groups.label: Source
groups.description: ProtonDB CDN reports currently carry slightly more confidence than native Pulse reports until the Pulse corpus grows. This penalty is temporary.

groups.label: Old-borked staleness flag
groups.description: Borked reports older than BORKED_DECAY_DAYS no longer auto-bump to bronze (that conflated rating with confidence). Instead, old borked reports drop their confidence by BORKED_STALENESS_PENALTY and surface a 'worth re-testing' caveat in the UI.

rating.description: Each report's rating contributes a numeric score (platinum=1.0, ... borked=0.0) weighted by report age. The final number maps back to the closest tier.

factors: Sample size - more reports raise confidence on a log scale (1 -> 30%, 5 -> 60%, 20+ -> 85%, 50+ -> 95%)
Freshness of newest report - a report from the last 6 months keeps confidence high
Consensus - tight agreement (low spread between report ratings) lifts confidence; mixed signal lowers it
Hardware match (signed-in only) - average of per-report hardware-match contributions

reportScoreTiers._note: Thresholds for the per-report confidence score shown in the Report Analysis modal. Max is ~175 (platinum + all bonuses + best-case multipliers). Used by both the plugin and web app.

tiers.label: Excellent
tiers.description: Platinum or gold report, recent, from hardware close to yours. High weight in confidence.

tiers.label: Good
tiers.description: Solid report with strong rating and reasonable hardware/recency match.

tiers.label: Fair
tiers.description: Older report, mismatched hardware, or lower rating. Still counts but with less weight.

tiers.label: Low
tiers.description: Borked, very old, or hardware mismatch. Minimal contribution to overall confidence.
